title: salvia bogotensis subsp. bogotensis
intro: Salvia bogotensis subsp. bogotensis, also known as Bogota sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sage family (Lamiaceae) native to the Andean region of South America. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin irritations, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune system. Herbal preparations of Salvia bogotensis subsp. bogotensis, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and utilization of its health benefits.
